name: Freightos
what_it_does: Digital freight marketplace API for instant rate retrieval, booking, and tracking across ocean, air, rail, and road freight modes including LCL, FCL, and air cargo.
use_cases: Instant freight rate comparison across carriers and forwarders
Automated freight booking for international shipments
Shipment tracking across ocean and air freight modes
Quote generation for supply chain procurement workflows
Freight spend analytics and carrier performance tracking
best_when: An agent needs to automate international freight procurement — comparing rates across forwarders and booking FCL/LCL/air cargo without manual quoting.
avoid_when: Your shipments are parcel-sized or you need domestic last-mile delivery capabilities.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials for platform integrations. API keys available for simpler integrations. Scopes cover rates, booking, and tracking separately.

pricing.notes: Pricing model depends on partnership type — platform integrators typically pay per booking or revenue share. Direct API access requires commercial agreement.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses are functional but inconsistent. Freight domain complexity means many failure modes relate to carrier availability or commodity restrictions that are not well documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Booking operations are not safe to retry without state tracking.
known_agent_gotchas: Rate quotes expire quickly (often 15-30 minutes) — agents must book promptly after quoting
Freight rates depend on commodity type, HS codes, and hazmat status — missing data causes silent failures
Origin/destination must use LOCODE format, not free-text addresses
Carrier availability varies significantly by trade lane — 'no results' is a valid API response
Booking confirmation is async — agents must poll or use webhooks for final status
Commercial agreement with Freightos required before sandbox access is granted

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 with scopes provides reasonable security foundation. GDPR compliance documented. Sandbox requires commercial agreement, limiting easy security testing.
